titleStmt.title: Memorandum by Mr. Paul H. Nitze, Deputy to the Assistant Secretary of State for Economic Affairs ( (Thorp), ), to the Director of the Office of Far Eastern Affairs ( (Butterworth))

div.head: Memorandum by Mr. Paul H. Nitze, Deputy to the Assistant Secretary of State for Economic Affairs ( Thorp ), to the Director of the Office of Far Eastern Affairs ( Butterworth )
div.p: I suggest that you read the attached memorandumOnly final portion printed, below; see memorandum of January 25, p. 623. on “The Hutchinson Reports on the Japanese Deconcentration Program” which appears to me to make a great deal of sense.

frus:attachment.p:
This Department recommends that: (1)
The DRB should be instructed to broaden the principles which it has adopted for the identification of excessive concentrations, in order to insure that: (a)
groups of companies related by horizontal ties are separated, wherever the economic power of the group is such as to constitute a threat to the development of competitive enterprise; and (b)
when a single company consisting of a number of technologically unrelated lines constitutes a threat to the development of competitive enterprise because of the cumulative economic power of these lines, such company is designated as an excessive concentration.
(2)
After such changes are made in the principles of implementation as are necessitated by (1) above, the DRB should assist the HCLC and the FTC to assimilate and carry out the program satisfactorily. It should then promptly withdraw from any further interference in the decisions of these Japanese agencies, in accordance with the requirements of NSC 13/2. (3)
Any revisions in Public Law 54 should be carefully drafted in the light of the need, not only for the prevention of restraints on competition in any one field of industry, but also for the prevention of the regrowth of the Zaibatsu form of control.
